mechanism_narrative: Declining payroll employment (PAYEMS) signals labor market deterioration that reduces consumer spending capacity and corporate earnings expectations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days. The mechanism operates through reduced aggregate demand and profit margin compression.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 130242k as of July 2010 reflects ongoing post-crisis labor slack
failure_condition: Monthly PAYEMS declines do not precede negative 20-day SPX returns in > 55% of instances over any 2-year rolling window, or Rank IC < 0.04 when lagged 1-2 months.

mechanism_narrative: The federal funds rate at near-zero levels (FEDFUNDS = 0.18%) combined with sustained low inflation (CPIAUCSL growth < 2% annualized) signals a monetary accommodation regime that supports equity valuations through low discount rates and positive real yields on equities relative to bonds.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS remains below 0.5% AND CPIAUCSL 12-month growth < 2.5%
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 0.18% and CPIAUCSL at 217.597 as of July 2010
regime_hint: zero-bound monetary accommodation with subdued inflation
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60-day returns in > 50% of periods when both conditions are met, or the correlation between (FEDFUNDS + CPIAUCSL growth) and forward SPX returns is not significantly positive (p > 0.10) over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) measure forward-looking construction demand and household formation expectations. Rising HOUST signals confidence in residential investment and consumer balance sheet health, driving positive equity returns through multiplier effects on employment and durable goods demand.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 549k units as of June 2010, well below pre-crisis trend
failure_condition: Month-over-month HOUST increases > 5% do not precede positive 20-day SPX returns in > 60% of instances, or Rank IC < 0.05 when lagged 0-1 months.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) reflects real economic capacity utilization and manufacturing momentum. Declining INDPRO signals demand weakness and inventory destocking, which compresses corporate profit margins and equity valuations over the subsequent 5-20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 92.5354 as of June 2010, recovering from crisis lows
failure_condition: Month-over-month INDPRO declines > 0.5% do not precede negative 5-day SPX returns in > 55% of instances, or Rank IC < 0.03 when lagged 0 months.

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales (RSAFS) directly measure consumer spending, the largest component of aggregate demand. A sustained decline in RSAFS signals demand destruction that reduces corporate revenues and equity earnings expectations, producing negative forward returns when combined with elevated unemployment (UNRATE > 9%).
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 362688M and UNRATE at 9.5% as of July 2010
regime_hint: post-crisis labor slack with fragile consumer demand
failure_condition: Joint condition (RSAFS decline > 1% AND UNRATE > 9%) does not precede negative 20-day SPX returns in > 58% of instances, or Rank IC < 0.05 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: The unemployment rate (UNRATE) at 9.5% signals persistent labor market slack that constrains wage growth and consumer confidence. When UNRATE remains elevated (> 9%) while payroll employment (PAYEMS) shows month-over-month stagnation, equity markets price in prolonged demand weakness and reduced earnings growth.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 9.5% and PAYEMS at 130242k as of July 2010
regime_hint: jobless recovery with weak labor market momentum
failure_condition: Joint condition (UNRATE > 9% AND PAYEMS stagnation) does not precede negative 60-day SPX returns in > 60% of instances, or the interaction term coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in logistic regression over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Real interest rates (approximated by FEDFUNDS minus CPIAUCSL inflation) at deeply negative levels (0.18% - ~1.5% annualized CPI growth = -1.3%) create a financial repression regime that forces equity allocation through yield-seeking behavior. This mechanism produces positive SPX returns as long as real rates remain negative.
evidence_refs: fact:Negative real rates at decision point support equity valuations
regime_hint: financial repression with negative real rates
failure_condition: Periods with negative real rates (FEDFUNDS - CPI growth < -0.5%) do not produce positive 60-day SPX returns in > 62% of instances, or Rank IC < 0.06 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) combined with payroll employment (PAYEMS) measures real economic momentum. When both series show month-over-month growth > 0.3%, it signals broad-based expansion that supports equity valuations; when either declines, it signals demand weakness that produces negative forward returns.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO month-over-month growth > 0.3% AND PAYEMS month-over-month growth > 0.1%
evidence_refs: fact:Both series show recovery momentum from crisis lows
regime_hint: early-cycle expansion with synchronized real activity growth
failure_condition: Joint condition (INDPRO > 0.3% AND PAYEMS > 0.1% monthly growth) does not precede positive 20-day SPX returns in > 65% of instances, or Rank IC < 0.07 over any 2-year rolling window.
